entities.name: FOOD LEGUMES
entities.description: Protein-rich crops usually cultivated as rain-dependent crops under subsistence agriculture

entities.name: RAIN-DEPENDENT
entities.description: Environmental condition where crops rely on rainfall for growth

entities.name: SUBSISTENCE AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Farming system where farmers grow food primarily for their own use

entities.name: IMPROVED SEEDS
entities.description: Seeds developed through breeding to possess superior agricultural traits

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area, often poor in legumes at the farm level

entities.name: HYBRIDS
entities.description: Offspring of two different varieties or species, often resulting in increased productivity

entities.name: INBRED CULTIVARS
entities.description: Pure-breeding lines currently in cultivation for legumes

entities.name: HYBRID BREEDING TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: The process of developing hybrid varieties to achieve a quantum jump in productivity

entities.name: FABA BEAN
entities.description: A food legume where hybrid breeding technology is being developed

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A food legume where commercial hybrids have been successfully bred

entities.name: NATURAL OUT-CROSSING
entities.description: The natural process of pollination between different plants, supporting hybrid seed production

entities.name: HYBRID SEED PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of producing seeds for hybrid crops

entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: A food legume where hybrid technology is being developed using male sterility and enhanced cross-pollination

entities.name: MALE STERILITY SYSTEMS
entities.description: Genetic systems bred into crops to prevent pollen production and facilitate hybridization

entities.name: CROSS-POLLINATION
entities.description: The transfer of pollen from one plant to another to facilitate the production of cross-pollinated seed

entities.name: COMMERCIAL HYBRIDS
entities.description: Hybrid varieties of pigeonpea that produce 30-50% more yield over local controls
